title: Rebus (Galactic Empire)
content:

{{Quote|…you shoulda' seen the results of our tests on Fest—my weapons are gonna put a lot of holes in a lot of Rebel scum—.|Moff Rebus|<ref name="DF" />}}
'''Rebus''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] at the start of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He was a noted weapons designer and created the prototype weapons for the Empire's [[Dark Trooper/Legends|dark trooper]] [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]]. He chose the sewage systems of [[Anoat/Legends|Anoat]] as his hideout and installed a sophisticated series of defenses to prevent intruders from reaching his lab. However, while investigating the [[Dark Trooper Project]] for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], the [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Kyle Katarn]] was able to [[Mission to Anoat|infiltrate]] Rebus's base and capture the Moff. During his interrogation, Rebus revealed information that helped Katarn track down the source of the dark [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] and eliminate them.

==Biography==
{{Quote|What kind of scum builds doomsday weapons for the Empire? I remember hearing about Moff Rebus during my training days. Everyone said he was quirky, and some said he was a genius. But how smart can you be if you live in a sewer system? Maybe he had an affinity for dianogas.|Kyle Katarn|<ref name="DF:OPG">''[[Dark Forces Official Player's Guide]]''</ref>}}
The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Rebus gained a reputation as a weapons designer during the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] leading up to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] and, by [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], held the rank of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Around that [[Time/Legends|time]] he operated out of a hidden stronghold, deep within the subterranean sewage system under [[Anoat City]],<ref name="DF" /> the long-abandoned sole settlement on [[Anoat/Legends|Anoat]] in the [[Anoat sector/Legends|Anoat sector]].<ref name="Galactic Gazetteer">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20040325web|text=Galactic Gazetteer: Hoth and the Greater Javin|int=Galactic Gazetteer: Hoth and the Greater Javin}}</ref>

The sewage system itself was mammoth and extremely confusing to navigate, all the while being virtually pitch black, making the use of a [[Head lamp/Legends|head lamp]] or [[Infrared goggles/Legends|infrared goggles]] imperative. Rebus made sure that even the smartest of trespassers were prevented from finding the route to his lab through the largely unexplored array of [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]] sewage tunnels by engineering a complicated series of drain gate switches. While the [[Dianoga/Legends|dianogas]] that infested the sewers provided a natural defense against intruders, Rebus also surrounded himself with [[Viper probe droid/Legends|Viper probe droids]], [[IT-O interrogation droid/Legends|IT-O Interrogator]] [[Droid/Legends|droids]], and [[Remote/Legends|remote]] droids, ready to attack any trespassers in various areas within the sewer complex. Several [[Gran/Legends|Gran]] thugs, armed with [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonators]], were also employed to provide further protection.<ref name="DF" />

[[File:AnoatSewer.png|thumb|left|270px|The sewers of Anoat City, which concealed Rebus's lab]]
Around the time of the Battle of [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]], Rebus designed the prototype weapons for the Empire's [[Dark Trooper/Legends|dark troopers]], a series of advanced [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] developed under [[General/Legends|General]] [[Rom Mohc]]. The dark trooper weapons, which bore the initials [[Mern/Legends|M]].[[Resh/Legends|R]]. on the barrel as his signature, were tested on [[Fest/Legends|Fest]] in the [[Atrivis sector/Legends|Atrivis sector]], and Rebus was pleased with the results. The weapons were used to devastating effect by dark troopers during the Imperial [[Attack on Tak Base|attack]] on the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic's]] [[Tak Base|base]] on [[Talay/Legends|Talay]]. Tak Base was destroyed within minutes and many innocent inhabitants of Talay were killed along with the Rebels. In the aftermath of the attack, Rebel agent [[Kyle Katarn]] [[Mission to Talay|traveled]] to Talay to investigate and discovered a prototype dark trooper weapon. When Katarn's partner, Rebel agent [[Jan Ors]], discovered Rebus's signature on the device, it led the pair to Anoat in search of the Moff.<ref name="DF" />

Katarn [[Mission to Anoat|proceeded]] into the sewer system and was able to successfully activate the drain gate switches and navigate to the Moff's lab. He found Rebus in his hideout, simply sitting in a brown chair holding a large weapon. Rebus put up no resistance whatsoever, and was captured and subsequently questioned on the [[Dark Trooper Project]]. Though he was uncooperative during questioning, providing the Rebels with little useful information, Rebus mentioned the results of the tests on Fest during one of his rants. The existence of an Imperial [[Weapons Research Facility]] on Fest was soon confirmed by the Rebel [[Spy/Legends|spy]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]], and Katarn [[Mission to Fest (Galactic Civil War)|proceeded]] to Fest to search for clues. He recovered a sample of [[Phrik/Legends|phrik]] from Fest, which was used in the construction of dark troopers and helped him to track down their source. Katarn eventually [[Mission to the Arc Hammer|eliminated]] Mohc and put an end to the dark troopers.<ref name="DF" />

==Personality and traits==
Moff Rebus was an eccentric and paranoid man who secluded himself in a hidden stronghold away from potential enemies.<ref name="DF" /> His quirky nature was well known to those within the Empire,<ref name="DF:OPG" /> and he gained a degree of notoriety due to his work as a weapons designer.<ref name="DF" /> Though he was considered by some to be a genius,<ref name="DF:OPG" /> he was also arrogant, proudly displaying his M.R. signature on weapons he designed, and later threatening the Rebels during the interrogation that followed his capture by Katarn and Ors. He was extremely loyal to the Empire and made no attempt to hide his dislike for the Rebel Alliance. He had light skin and [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and a rich brown moustache.<ref name="DF" />

==Equipment==
Rebus was armed with a large weapon, which he was using while wearing a white undershirt instead of an [[Imperial military uniforms/Legends|Imperial uniform]] as he was found and captured by Katarn.<ref name="DF" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|I.M. stands for "Imperial Machines," an outside contractor of Imperial weapons. I think Moff Rebus did some work for them but he hated their political infighting, and he wanted a more creative environment.|Justin Chin|<ref name="FAQ">{{WebCite|url=http://members.shaw.ca/kloepel/DarkForces/dftext2.html#DF51|text=Dark Forces FAQ|author=Karsten A. Loepelmann|archivedate=20130808065136}}</ref>}}
Rebus was created for the [[1995]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]''. His only appearance in the game is during the third mission, "Anoat City: The Subterranean Hideout," in which players, taking the role of Kyle Katarn, are charged with locating and capturing Rebus. The character does not speak in the game and is captured without any resistance, despite being depicted holding a weapon.<ref name="DF" />

[[Justin Chin]], the writer and lead artist for ''Dark Forces'', has stated that Rebus once did some work for [[Imperial Machines]], but sought a more creative environment and disliked the [[Politics/Legends|political]] infighting at the company. However, this has not been confirmed in any [[canon]] source.<ref name="FAQ" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]'' {{1st}}
*''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces Remaster]]''
